Title: Benzoic and salicylic acids inhibit β-substituted alanine synthase 4;1 in common bean

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: β-Substituted alanine synthase 4;1 / O-acetylserine sulfhydrylase / cysteine synthase
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent enzyme; type II fold O-acetylserine sulfhydrylase/cytosolic clade II cysteine synthase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cytosolic de novo cysteine synthesis branch; probable S-methylcysteine-forming branch
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream terminal sulfur-incorporation step from O-acetylserine; branch point for cysteine versus S-methylcysteine formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses O-acetylserine with sulfide to form cysteine; evidence further supports condensation of O-acetylserine with methanethiol to form free S-methylcysteine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major cytosolic cysteine synthase isoform in developing seeds; key control point supported by in vivo inhibitor suppression of cysteine and downstream sulfur metabolites, but formal rate-limiting designation not established
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms cysteine and likely directly forms free S-methylcysteine, which feeds downstream γ-glutamyl-S-methylcysteine and S-methylhomoglutathione
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Amino acid; sulfur-containing amino acid and derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritionally important sulfur amino acid metabolism in legumes; relevant to improving seed sulfur amino acid composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PvBSAS4;1 promotes cysteine biosynthesis in developing common bean seeds and likely contributes to free S-methylcysteine formation; benzoic acid and salicylic acid inhibit its activity, reducing isotope incorporation into cysteine and downstream sulfur metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays; crystal structure determination; in vitro inhibition kinetics; isotope-feeding in developing cotyledons with 13C3,15N-serine; 13C-methanethiol precursor tracing by LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Major isoform of cytosolic cysteine synthase present in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro benzoic acid and salicylic acid treatment; in vivo benzoic acid treatment (1.2 mM) in cultured developing cotyledons; isotope feeding with 13C3,15N-serine or 13C-labeled sodium thiomethoxide
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by high-resolution LC-MS/MS; structural biology data PDB 9RJ1; raw X-ray diffraction DOI 10.60884/BCOLCQ
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for engineering seed sulfur amino acid composition in common bean; manipulation should consider dual effects on cysteine and S-methylcysteine branches and possible use of inhibitor-binding knowledge for metabolic modulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inhibition reduces cysteine, γ-glutamylcysteine, homoglutathione, γ-glutamyl-S-methylcysteine, and S-methylhomoglutathione labeling, indicating broad impacts on sulfur metabolite pools
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure unexpectedly contained benzoic acid near the active site, likely co-purified from E. coli; benzoic acid competitively inhibits with respect to O-acetylserine; salicylic acid also inhibits with similar IC50 (~0.6 mM); enzyme is a homodimer with two active sites; possible adaptation for methanethiol processing inferred from active-site channel residues
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvBSAS4;1 is a PLP-dependent cytosolic clade II cysteine synthase and the major developing-seed isoform in common bean. 2. Structural analysis identified benzoic acid bound near the active site in a position overlapping the O-acetylserine-binding region. 3. Recombinant enzyme kinetics showed benzoic acid is a competitive inhibitor versus O-acetylserine, and salicylic acid also inhibits with IC50 ≈ 0.6 mM. 4. In developing cotyledons, benzoic acid reduced labeled serine incorporation into cysteine, γ-glutamylcysteine, and homoglutathione by about half. 5. Benzoic acid completely abolished labeling of S-methylhomoglutathione and γ-glutamyl-S-methylcysteine. 6. 13C-thiomethoxide feeding supported a role for PvBSAS4;1 in formation of free S-methylcysteine from O-acetylserine and methanethiol.
